openFindings.title: Backfill still defaults to archive-inclusive traversal
openFindings.impact: The runtime and tests still treat archive-inclusive traversal as the default. If this packet's intended production behavior is active-only by default with explicit archive opt-in, the shipped implementation has the wrong default semantics and the regression suite protects the opposite behavior.
openFindings.remediation: Decide the intended contract. If active-only default is correct, initialize activeOnly=true, make --include-archive set it false, and invert the default traversal tests.

openFindings.title: Active-only archive detection uses the absolute path instead of a root-relative path
openFindings.impact: In active-only mode, any parent directory segment named z_archive or z_future above the specs root would cause traversal to return early before inspecting active packets.
openFindings.remediation: Evaluate archive segments on a path relative to the traversal root, or pass root into the predicate and skip only descendants below archive/future segments.

openFindings.title: CLI argument parsing silently accepts missing values and unknown flags
openFindings.impact: A mistyped flag is ignored, and a bare --root falls back to the default specs root. Operators can get a successful dry-run or write run over the wrong corpus instead of an actionable usage error.
openFindings.remediation: Reject unknown arguments and require a following non-flag value for --root.

openFindings.title: Tests cover direct API options but not the CLI flag contract
openFindings.impact: The tests exercise collectSpecFolders/runBackfill options but do not verify parseArgs or CLI behavior for --active-only, --include-archive, missing --root values, or unknown flags.
openFindings.remediation: Expose a small argument parser helper for tests or add CLI-level subprocess tests for the supported flags and failure cases.

rejectedFindings.reason: Doc-only metadata drift was intentionally out of scope for this second pass.
